summary.intent: Address 5 PR review comments (2 P1 security/correctness issues and 3 P2 issues) raised on PR #730 in the moltis repo.
summary.outcome: All 5 issues fixed across 4 files, 108 tests passing (+1 new test), code formatted and linted clean, changes committed and pushed.

repo: The moltis repo uses worktrees under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/fork-piper/
Crate structure: crates/matrix (Rust OIDC logic), crates/gateway (Rust HTTP layer), crates/web (JS frontend)
The `url` crate is already a dependency of the gateway crate — no Cargo.toml change needed for URL encoding
Frontend JS files use Preact signals (useSignal) in page-channels.js but useState in onboarding-view.js — both already import useRef

code.finding: PersistedOidcSession had #[derive(Debug)] which could leak tokens in logs; replaced with manual Debug impl that redacts sensitive fields. Also had blocking std::fs I/O inside async functions — replaced with tokio::fs.
Callback URL was constructed by string concatenation, enabling URL parameter injection. Fixed by using the url crate to properly encode query parameters.

OIDC polling interval ID was not stored, so setInterval could not be cleared on modal close/unmount. Fixed by storing interval ID in a useRef and clearing it in resetForm and the modal's onClose handler.
Same polling interval leak as page-channels.js. Fixed with useRef to store and clear the interval ID.
panic! was used in production code path in build_client_metadata; replaced with proper error propagation.

workflow: New test added alongside the fix (debug_impl_redacts_tokens) to prevent regression on the token redaction behavior
Compilation, tests, rustfmt, and clippy all run before committing — standard gate before pushing fixes

friction: The polling interval fix in page-channels.js required multiple sequential edits (storing the ref, updating resetForm, updating the OIDC flow, updating onClose) — the modal close path was not immediately obvious and required a grep to locate
open_items: PR comments were fixed but the transcript does not show them being explicitly resolved/dismissed on GitHub — may need manual follow-up
No mention of updating CHANGELOG or PR description to document the security fixes (token exposure, URL injection)
